title: microlepia hirta
intro: Microlepia hirta, also known as the 'Hairy Polypody', is a type of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae and is native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Microlepia hirta include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of Microlepia hirta can be made into teas, infusions, or ointments, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
